title: @aws-sdk/lib-storage Upload and Resource Budgets · Compiler Case: Managed Upload Migration

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and TypeScript Predict Managed Upload Migration · Theory Managed Upload Migration
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory TypeScript file-delivery team handles case 4130 during a AWS SDK v3 dependency canary. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to bound @aws-sdk/lib-storage Upload workers, part buffers, abort ownership, and cleanup policy; separate deterministic browser evidence from controlled Node and AWS transfer gates; then double concurrent Upload instances and preserve aggregate memory and worker budgets. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. Node and AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, PostgreSQL, credentials, host state, handle and stream checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory v2 ManagedUpload and hand-written multipart behavior, map queue, part, progress, abort, cleanup, and result differences, then migrate to v3 Upload behind contract tests.
